summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms command injection in NetworkHealthCheck. A malicious host address containing shell metacharacters injected via broker.xml configuration triggers arbitrary command execution (touch /tmp/pwned; echo EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS) when the broker initializes network health checking.
expected_security_impact: Injecting shell metacharacters into the network health check host parameter should cause ProcessBuilder to execute arbitrary commands beyond the intended ping command, creating proof files and demonstrating RCE capability.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully executed the injected commands. The broker initialized, parsed the malicious network-check-URL-list containing ';touch /tmp/pwned;echo EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS > /tmp/proof.txt;#', and ProcessBuilder executed these commands as part of the network check process, creating /tmp/pwned and /tmp/proof.txt files.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the existence of /tmp/proof.txt and verifies it contains 'EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS', confirming that injected commands executed rather than just ping failures or syntax errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: /tmp/pwned file created by injected touch command
Broker logs show network health check executing with malformed host parameter
No ping-related errors indicating command injection bypassed normal validation

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones Apache ActiveMQ Artemis repository from GitHub, checks out tag 2.31.0, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
